exports.content: ACON (Agent Context Optimization) is a unified framework that compresses **both** environment observations and interaction histories into concise but informative condensations, applied selectively only when the corresponding length exceeds a threshold ($T_{hist}$ for history, $T_{obs}$ for observation).
exports.label: acon_compresses_history_and_obs

exports.content: The optimised compressor (a large LLM with the tuned guideline) can be distilled into a smaller compressor LLM while retaining most of the teacher's downstream agent accuracy, lowering deployment cost.
exports.label: acon_distillable

exports.content: ACON's compression-guideline optimisation operates entirely in natural language space (no parameter updates of the compressor LLM are required), making it directly applicable to closed-source / API-only models.
exports.label: acon_is_gradient_free

exports.content: ACON resolves the unbounded-context problem in long-horizon LLM agents by (i) compressing both history and observation only when their length exceeds a threshold (@history_compression_rule, @observation_compression_rule), (ii) optimising the natural-language guideline gradient-free via contrastive feedback (@textual_gradient_step), and (iii) distilling the optimised compressor into a small model (@distillation_decouples_inference) so the compressor's overhead does not dominate the savings.
exports.label: acon_solves_problem

exports.content: When ACON's optimised compressor (gpt-4.1 teacher) is distilled into smaller models (Qwen3-14B, Qwen3-8B, Phi-4), the student compressors retain **over 95%** of the teacher compressor's downstream agent accuracy.
exports.label: claim_distillation_preserves_accuracy

exports.content: ACON acts as an equaliser for smaller LLM agents: with concise but informative contexts, Qwen3-14B improves by up to **+32%** on AppWorld, **+20%** on OfficeBench and **+46%** on 8-objective QA versus the no-compression baseline.
exports.label: claim_helps_small_agents

exports.content: ACON reduces peak input tokens by **26-54%** across AppWorld, OfficeBench and 8-objective QA while largely preserving (and sometimes improving) task accuracy when paired with a strong agent (gpt-4.1).
exports.label: claim_peak_token_reduction

exports.content: Despite token-level savings, ACON's compressor module introduces extra API/compute cost. Figure 7 shows that **observation compression reduces total API cost** by trimming agent inputs, but **history compression rarely reduces dollar cost** in practice because the compressor's own LLM calls — and the loss of KV-cache reuse on the agent — offset the token savings. Distillation partly mitigates this by replacing expensive LLM compressor calls with cheaper smaller-model calls.
exports.label: cost_limitation

exports.content: Optimiser strength has a much larger effect on guideline quality than the contrastive feedback ablation: switching from o3 to gpt-4.1 as the optimiser drops accuracy by 3.6 points (51.2 -> 47.6), while switching to gpt-5 drops it by only 0.6.
exports.label: strong_optimizer_matters

exports.content: **Moderate thresholds** ($T_{hist}=4096$, $T_{obs}=1024$) are recommended as the best accuracy/efficiency trade-off on AppWorld with gpt-4.1.
exports.label: threshold_recommendation
